mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 55% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero average 60-day forward return, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term equity price appreciation over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are uncorrelated with INDPRO momentum during early-cycle phases, or the signal generates negative alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a suppressed federal funds rate compresses real yields and signals delayed monetary tightening, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation/low-rate environment produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive directional bias following payroll increases, or the Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, which forces equity markets to price in severe earnings contractions and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 0% for two consecutive periods
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in > 50% of confirmed recession quarters, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate signal a synchronized supply-labor recovery, which drives rapid short-term equity repricing over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative average 5-day return, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy by at least 5 percentage points.
